title: Learning to Learn
objective: Students will explore learning to learn concepts including Finding Knowledge Gaps, Setting Learning Goals.

steps.content: Let's discover the amazing world of Finding Knowledge Gaps!
Survey your own understanding of a whole topic — identify where your knowledge is solid, where it is shaky, and what still needs work
Let me show you something cool. metacognitive monitoring research
If you ask you how well they understand a whole topic, can they tell you honestly which parts they feel confident on and which parts need more work?
Great job! Today we learned about Finding Knowledge Gaps. metacognitive monitoring research Flavell metacognition framework
Today we're going to learn about Setting Learning Goals. Get ready for an adventure!
Set a specific learning goal, work towards it with a deliberate plan, then honestly assess whether you achieved it and what you would change
Time to try this out: Set a specific, achievable learning goal for themselves — e.g. 'By Friday I want to know all my 7-times tables' rather than just 'get better at maths'
Does you ever set themselves a specific learning goal — like 'I want to get better at times tables by the end of the week' — and then honestly judge whether they met it?

You did it! Here's what we explored about Setting Learning Goals: Set a specific, achievable learning goal for themselves — e.g. 'By Friday I want to know all my 7-times tables' rather than just 'get better at maths' Make a simple plan for how to reach their goal and follow through on it over several days
